items.headline: CBP CAPE System 85% Complete - April 20 Launch Confirmed by CIT
items.summary: Court of International Trade Judge Richard Eaton confirmed CBP is "on track to meet the April 20, 2026 deadline" for Phase 1 CAPE launch. System components now 60-85% complete with claim portal leading at 85%. CBP must file next status report April 14 for closed conference review. This marks final confirmation of imminent launch timeline.
items.source: Thompson Hine SmarTrade / CIT Order April 1, 2026

items.headline: Phase 1 Covers 63% of IEEPA Entries - 45-Day Processing Confirmed
items.summary: CBP's March 31 declaration reveals Phase 1 will process approximately 63% of IEEPA entries including unliquidated entries and those within 90-day voluntary reliquidation period. Processing time confirmed at up to 45 days from acceptance unless compliance concerns trigger further review. Finally liquidated entries deferred to Phase 2 with no timeline.
items.source: Multiple law firm analyses of CBP March 31 Declaration

items.headline: 92% of Eligible Importers Still Not ACH-Enrolled for Electronic Refunds
items.summary: Only 26,664 of 330,000 eligible importers have completed ACE+ACH enrollment required for electronic refunds. CBP confirmed all refunds will be electronic-only with no paper checks. Unenrolled importers will have refunds rejected and placed in reject status until enrollment completed. $23M in statutory interest accrues daily.

items.headline: CAPE System Expanded to Include Suspended/Extended/Under Review Entries
items.summary: CBP expanded Phase 1 scope beyond unliquidated entries to include those with liquidation status of 'Suspended,' 'Extended,' or 'Under Review.' For these entries, IEEPA duties will be removed but refunds delayed until normal liquidation completion. Entries with AD/CVD complications and drawback claims remain excluded from Phase 1.

items.headline: CIT Judge Endorses CBP Progress - No Appeal Deadline Extension
items.summary: Judge Eaton issued order confirming CBP makes "satisfactory progress" on CAPE development and endorsed April 20 launch timeline. Court rejected any delays and maintained pressure on CBP to deliver. Government appeal deadline for CIT's broad refund order remains May 4, 2026, creating urgency for importers to preserve rights.
items.source: Court of International Trade Order April 1, 2026

items.headline: Finally Liquidated Entries Still Uncertain Despite CIT March 27 Order
items.summary: While CIT ordered CBP to process finally liquidated entries, CBP's March 31 declaration confirms these are excluded from Phase 1 and deferred to future phase with no timeline. Legal uncertainty remains whether CBP has authority to reliquidate beyond 90-day window. Importers advised to continue filing protests to preserve rights.
items.source: Kelley Drye / Troutman Pepper Analysis March 31, 2026
